events.title: Former Democratic presidents remember the late Rev. Jesse Jackson during final public tribute
events.description: “He paved the road for so many others to follow,” former President Barack Obama said of the civil rights legend.

events.title: Dems aren’t sure whether to actually spend big to flip Texas
events.description: National Democrats are worried the money it would take to compete in Texas could be better spent elsewhere.

events.title: Top Mamdani aide takes progressive project to the UK
events.description: Morris Katz has been advising British progressives, showing how left-wing movements around the world are looking at Mamdani’s playbook for replication.

events.title: The nation’s cartoonists on the week in politics
events.description: Every week political cartoonists throughout the country and across the political spectrum apply their ink-stained skills to capture the foibles, memes, hypocrisies and other head-slapping events in th

events.title: War, Trump and Washington’s Gridlock | Sen. Katie Britt
Republican group attacks Thomas Massie for his opposition to Iran war
events.description: An ad supporting the lawmaker’s rival aired hours after the House rejected a War Powers resolution.

events.title: 'Good riddance': Dems cheer Noem’s ouster — and call for more departures
events.description: Democratic lawmakers said Stephen Miller and Pam Bondi should be next after President Donald Trump announced Kristi Noem would be stepping aside as DHS secretary.

events.title: ‘This L is on her’: Black lawmakers and strategists dump on Crockett
events.description: “She has been and has felt like a woman on an island,” says one operative.

events.title: Nervous Republicans rally behind Cornyn as they wait on Trump’s Texas pick
events.description: John Cornyn and his allies are trying to turn a surprising lead over firebrand Ken Paxton into a decisive presidential nod.

events.title: Texas Latinos turned out in massive numbers for Democrats
events.description: In multiple Latino-majority counties, more people cast Democratic votes on Tuesday than voted for Kamala Harris in 2024.

events.title: ‘A perfect storm is lining up for Texas Democrats’
events.description: James Talarico prevailed in a hard-fought Democratic contest. Now he waits to see if he'll face scandal-plagued Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton — or four-term Sen. John Cornyn.

events.title: Cornyn is still in the fight. Will Trump finally endorse him?
events.description: Even in the most heavily Republican counties where Attorney General Ken Paxton might have expected to benefit from a MAGA base, incumbent Sen. John Cornyn largely held his own.

events.title: Talarico defeats Crockett in Texas Senate Democratic primary
events.description: He took a big-tent approach to his campaign by appealing to voters from both parties and independents.

events.title: Scandal-plagued Rep. Tony Gonzales forced to runoff
events.description: Gonzales, who was accused of having an affair with a staffer who later died by suicide, will face influencer Brandon Herrera in a May 26 runoff.

events.title: Rep. Dan Crenshaw ousted by primary challenger to his right
events.description: State Rep. Steve Toth cast him as insufficiently conservative for the Texas district. President Donald Trump declined to endorse.

events.title: Cornyn, Paxton head to runoff in Texas Senate GOP race
events.description: Sen. John Cornyn is in the fight of his career — and national Republicans fear they could lose the seat if he falls.

events.title: Rep. Chip Roy headed to runoff in Texas AG race
events.description: Trump withheld an endorsement in the race to replace Ken Paxton.

events.title: Ties to Israel plague Democrats in top primaries post-Gaza
events.description: In Illinois, Texas and Michigan, candidates have been forced to address connections to Israel as support wanes among Democrats.

events.title: MAGA loves scandal-ridden Ken Paxton. Can they get him to the Senate?
events.description: The Texas attorney general will face off against longtime senator John Cornyn in a state that’s increasingly welcomed far-right causes.

events.title: Centrist Dems met to plot 2028. Then Iran happened.
events.description: At a gathering this week, top center-left consultants and strategists for rumored presidential hopefuls pitched how to talk about foreign policy.
